title: 36" Sink Base Kitchen Cabinet
summary: A base cabinet built without a fixed bottom shelf where the sink and plumbing go, keeping the same carcass system as the rest of the run.
description:
A sink base looks almost identical to a standard base cabinet from the front, but the interior is deliberately left more open — the bottom shelf area needs to clear a sink bowl and drain plumbing, so a full shelf there is unlikely to be useful and is treated as optional in this plan. What isn't optional is keeping the outside dimensions and face-frame reveal identical to the rest of your base-cabinet run, so the counter line and toe-kick stay continuous once everything is installed.

The back panel gets a genuine exception here: it's fine to skip it entirely to leave room for supply lines and a drain trap, but the plan still calls for adding small strips of 1/4" plywood at the top and bottom of the back opening. That's not decorative — it brings the cabinet's back edge out 1/4" to match the depth of the neighboring cabinets that do have full backs, so the counter overhang stays consistent across the whole run even where this one cabinet is open behind.

tools.note: Handy for any plumbing cutouts in the back or shelf, if you choose to add one.
For ripping the plywood carcass parts to width.

materials.name: Plywood, 1/4", 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Optional back — can be omitted entirely for plumbing clearance, keeping only small strips top and bottom.

cutList.note: Unlikely to be useful on a sink base — the sink bowl and plumbing generally need this space clear.

steps.title: Build the side panels in mirror
steps.body: Build the two side panels as true mirror images of each other, matching the outside dimensions of every other base cabinet in your run.

steps.title: Attach the bottom supports
steps.body: Attach the two bottom-support strips to the two side panels.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf and second side
steps.body: Attach the bottom shelf to the two bottom supports and one side panel, then attach the remaining side panel to complete the base.

steps.title: Add the top supports
steps.body: Attach the top supports across the front and back of the carcass.

steps.title: Attach the back — or leave it open for plumbing
steps.body: Attach the back panel with 3/4" finish nails and glue. On a sink base, the back can be left off entirely to leave room for supply lines and a drain trap — if you do, still add small strips of 1/4" plywood at the top and bottom of the opening, which brings the cabinet's back edge out 1/4" to match cabinets with full backs.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame separately, then attach the completed frame to the front of the cabinet. Sides overextend by 1/4" on each side; the top is flush; the bottom is flush at the sides with the bottom shelf set up 1/2".

steps.title: Hang doors and drawer front
steps.body: Install the two full-overlay doors and the drawer front using concealed euro hinges with a 1-1/4" overlay.

images.alt: Fitted kitchen sink base cabinet with a face frame, doors, and open plumbing clearance behind
